topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The clinking of champagne glasses and the vibrant chatter of the reception washed over me, a warm, enveloping tide. I leaned against a marble pillar, a half-empty flute of prosecco cooling in my hand, observing the joyous chaos. Then, across the crowded dance floor, a familiar silhouette caught my eye. It was Sarah. She was laughing, her head tilted back, a genuine, unrestrained sound escaping her. A slow, pleasant warmth bloomed in my chest, smoothing out any lingering tension I hadn't realized I was carrying. I smiled, a soft, unforced curve of my lips, and simply watched her for a moment longer, feeling perfectly settled in the middle of the celebration.

[story 2]

I found myself drifting toward the terrace overlooking the gardens, the evening air crisp and carrying the faint scent of honeysuckle. The noise of the wedding reception faded into a pleasant hum, a backdrop to the quiet contentment settling over me. I was scrolling through photos on my phone, a small, private smile playing on my face, when I spotted him near the edge of the foliage. He looked up, and for a brief instant, the world narrowed down to just that shared glance. A relaxed feeling washed over my limbs, a sense of familiarity and gentle acceptance. I took a slow, deliberate sip of water, feeling completely unburdened.

[story 3]

The music pulsed, a steady, rhythmic beat that seemed to sync perfectly with my own relaxed breathing. I was standing near the buffet, nursing a glass of sparkling cider, feeling wonderfully disconnected from the hurried pace of the day. Then, there he was—my old college acquaintance, standing near the entrance, looking surprisingly relaxed amidst the flurry of activity. When our eyes met, there was no shock, just a quiet recognition, a soft acknowledgment that settled deep in my core. I offered a small, genuine nod, a slight upturn of my mouth that spoke volumes without needing a single word. I let my shoulders drop, letting the atmosphere simply carry me.
